text:
To find the combined weight of the two pennies, we start by determining the weight of each penny individually.

First, we know the weight of the 1959 penny is 48 grains.

Next, we are told that the 2010 penny is three-quarters of the weight of the 1959 penny. So, we calculate the weight of the 2010 penny as follows:
\[
\text{Weight of 2010 penny} = \frac{3}{4} \times 48 \text{ grains} = 36 \text{ grains}
\]

Now we add the weights of the two pennies together:
\[
\text{Combined weight} = 48 \text{ grains} + 36 \text{ grains} = 84 \text{ grains}
\]

Thus, the combined weight of the two pennies is \(\boxed{84}\) grains.
